Effective recall: the denominator that always said 100%

The trigger gate could report PASS for a skill that barely triggered. Move the sliders to see why, and what the fix changes.

The two formulas

old  eff_recall = tp_partial / (tp_partial + fn)     // flaky mass on BOTH sides
new  eff_recall = tp_partial / pos_total             // full measured positive population

Why the old one breaks: tp_partial accumulates 1 per fully-passing prompt and the pass rate per flaky one. When nothing hard-misses, fn = 0, so the expression reduces to tp_partial / tp_partial = 100%, regardless of how badly the prompts actually scored.

Try it

Build a run out of should_trigger prompts: some pass every rep, some miss every rep, some are flaky.

Observed in CI run 29742402853

Both rows are reproduced exactly by the regression test in this PR, which keeps the old formula alongside the new one so it proves the fail-open is gone rather than just asserting today's numbers.

skillwhat happenedoldnew
ci-sentinel1 of 3 reps on its only prompt100.0 PASS33.0 FAIL
implement3 OK · 2 MISS · 3 flaky68.3 FAIL54.0 FAIL

No currently-failing skill flips green. The vacuous PASS flips to FAIL, which is the entire point.

A second dead gate, found on the way

tests/evals was never in CI's test-dir list. Four guards lived there passing locally and gating nothing:

test-quality-index-gate.sh
test-dead-generation-guard.sh   // #3036, the predecessor of this work
test-eval-coverage.sh
test-effective-recall.sh        // new in this PR

Same shape as the islands suite: a test file that runs green on a laptop and is invisible to CI. Now wired in; all five gate for real. They are pure logic and make no model calls.
